block_goal: Implement the СоСНА AI memory service with contracts, raw/safe corpus, graph/vector/full-text retrieval, RBAC enforcement, PII protection, and a declared memory.search tool.
architect_instructions_for_orchestrator: Use docs/planning/active/ai-memory-service.md as the active human-readable plan and this ARCHITECT_PLAN.json as the executable workflow plan.
Do not dispatch implementation tasks that introduce new architectural decisions unless ADR-0003 is updated or a new ADR is created first.
Keep each executor inside the declared write scope and do not let backend spike tasks modify production code.
Do not add generated indexes, evaluation outputs, model caches, or temporary experiment files outside data/ or .local/.
Do not start a dependent slice until prerequisite task acceptance is written.
global_rules: Django remains the system of record for business data, identity, policies, and audit.
Agent runtime accesses memory only through declared tools; it must not import memory internals or write directly to indexes.
Default contracts live in contracts/ and runtime copies live in data/contracts/.
Mutable memory state, raw snapshots, safe corpus, indexes, manifests, and eval outputs live under data/memory/.
Patient data may be indexed only after de-identification or stable pseudonym/ID replacement.
Cloud LLM routing for sensitive context is denied unless an explicit route policy later permits a safe redacted path.
Raw snapshots are immutable audit artifacts; hard update may deactivate active chunks/facts but must not delete raw history.
global_acceptance: The block passes make check, make test, and make contracts at completion.
Memory contracts are validated by validate_architecture_contracts.
The memory app records source, snapshot, chunk, graph fact, job, access audit, and eval metadata.
memory.search returns cited context and refuses forbidden scopes.
PII and secret leakage test suites pass with zero known leaks.
Generated runtime data does not appear in the repository root.
global_invariants: No direct SQL writes by agent runtime.
No patient PII in LLM prompts or tool traces.
No secrets in prompts, logs, tool traces, contracts, or committed files.
No new files directly under workflow/ root for this block; use workflow/active/block-ai-memory-service-2026-05-19/.
No production dependency on PostgreSQL in the first implementation block unless ADR-0003 is updated.

slices.title: Memory Contracts Foundation
slices.goal: Add memory contracts, schemas, settings loading, validators, and architecture contract checks without implementing indexes or runtime retrieval.
forbidden_moves: Do not implement apps.memory in this slice.
Do not add vector, graph, Presidio, Kuzu, LanceDB, or Qdrant dependencies in this slice.
Do not modify agent runtime or AI tool declarations in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: Memory contracts exist in contracts/ai and are copied to data/contracts/ai by existing contract path semantics.
Invalid memory source/profile/routing payloads fail validation.
validate_architecture_contracts validates the memory contract family.
PROJECT_STRUCTURE.yaml includes the new contract and schema files.

tasks.goal: Implement memory contracts, schemas, settings loading, and validators.
implementation_notes: Follow the existing get_contract_path pattern in config/settings.py.
Keep default contracts small and safe; no real patient data, secrets, or host-specific values.
Validators should be strict enough to catch missing required keys and invalid enum values before runtime.
Run make gen-struct after adding contract/schema files.
escalate_if: A contract field changes an accepted ADR decision
Runtime contract copy behavior needs a new storage pattern
Tests require broad unrelated changes

slices.title: Backend Technical Spikes
slices.goal: Evaluate graph/vector/backend choices without committing production code paths.
forbidden_moves: Do not modify production code in this spike task.
Do not commit model caches, generated indexes, or raw experiment data.
Do not add dependencies to requirements.txt from spike results without a follow-up accepted task.
required_checks: Document spike commands and outcomes in the executor report
acceptance_criteria: Spike report compares LanceDB and Qdrant against filtering, hybrid/full-text, local deployment, and operational complexity.
Spike report validates Kuzu graph schema feasibility.
Spike report states whether Graphiti should be used as an adapter or deferred.
Spike report recommends one vector backend for the first production implementation task.

tasks.goal: Run bounded backend spikes and write a recommendation report.
implementation_notes: Prefer disposable scripts/reports under .local/ for experiments.
If data/memory/eval is used, keep only operator-relevant evaluation artifacts and no patient data.
Record version assumptions and exact commands in the executor report.
escalate_if: A production dependency must be added before the spike can proceed
Graphiti requires a graph backend that conflicts with ADR-0003
The spike finds that embedded storage is not viable

slices.title: Django Memory App Scaffold
slices.goal: Create apps.memory with metadata models, policies, selectors, services, admin, migrations, and basic tests.
forbidden_moves: Do not implement index backends in this slice.
Do not alter existing business models unless a separate ADR/task packet approves it.
Do not modify agent runtime in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: apps.memory is registered and has metadata models with migrations.
Admin exposes memory sources, snapshots, chunks, graph facts, index jobs, access audit, and eval cases.
No indexing or LLM calls are performed by model methods.
Tests cover model invariants and basic policy/selectors behavior.

tasks.goal: Create the apps.memory scaffold and metadata model layer.
implementation_notes: Use BigAutoField defaults and separate external IDs only when needed.
Keep business logic in services.py, access checks in policies.py, and reads in selectors.py.
Use JSONField for backend-neutral metadata but keep query-critical fields as concrete model fields.
escalate_if: Metadata model requires PostgreSQL-specific fields
Existing app policies need broad changes
Migration conflicts with current database conventions

slices.title: De-identification and DLP Pipeline
slices.goal: Implement de-identification, deterministic pseudonymization, DLP scanning, blocked snapshot handling, and leakage regression tests.
forbidden_moves: Do not send PII to any LLM in tests or implementation.
Do not commit real patient examples.
Do not store pseudonymization secrets in contracts or code.
acceptance_criteria: Safe corpus generation removes or pseudonymizes patient identifiers.
Secret-like content blocks indexing.
Regression tests cover Russian PII examples using synthetic data.
Blocked snapshot reason is stored for operator review.

tasks.goal: Implement the safe-text and DLP layer for memory ingestion.
implementation_notes: Use synthetic Russian examples in tests.
Keep secret material behind env/deployment settings or future secret handles.
If adding Presidio dependencies, keep the dependency change isolated and documented.
escalate_if: Presidio model support for Russian is insufficient for acceptance criteria
A secret manager decision is needed before safe pseudonymization can proceed
Any test fixture would contain real PII

slices.title: Graph Vector Full-text Indexing
slices.goal: Implement graph and vector/full-text backend interfaces and the selected first backend implementations.
forbidden_moves: Do not index raw PII snapshots.
Do not make graph/vector backend imports mandatory at Django startup if optional dependencies are unavailable.
Do not bypass MemorySnapshot and MemoryChunk provenance.
acceptance_criteria: Graph facts and vector chunks are linked to safe chunks and source snapshots.
Index writes are idempotent by chunk/fact identity.
Old active chunks/facts can be deactivated without deleting raw snapshots.
Scope and sensitivity metadata are available to retrieval filters.

tasks.goal: Implement indexing interfaces and selected graph/vector backend implementations.
implementation_notes: Use backend interfaces so Kuzu/LanceDB/Qdrant choices remain replaceable.
Persist generated indexes under data/memory/indexes/ only.
Store index manifests under data/memory/manifests/.
escalate_if: Selected backend requires PostgreSQL or external services contrary to ADR-0003
Index filtering cannot enforce scope metadata
Generated indexes would need to be committed

slices.title: Memory Search Tool and Retrieval Orchestration
slices.goal: Implement memory.search as the sole agent runtime access path to memory, including scope translation, query planning, rank fusion, citations, and audit.
forbidden_moves: Do not let agent runtime write directly to memory indexes.
Do not expose raw snapshots through memory.search.
Do not return uncited memory context unless the tool explicitly reports no sources found.
acceptance_criteria: memory.search is declared in tool definitions and JSON contracts without drift.
Retrieval applies backend filtering and Django post-filtering.
Tool responses include citations, policy decision, and retrieval trace.
Forbidden-scope test cases return no leaked chunks/facts.

tasks.goal: Implement the memory.search retrieval tool and orchestration layer.
implementation_notes: Follow existing tool registry drift validation patterns.
Keep route decisions in server-side policy code, not in prompts.
Return compact cited context rather than full source documents.
escalate_if: Tool contract requires changing the AI identity model
Cloud route behavior needs a new ADR
Existing AI task type validation cannot represent memory.search

slices.title: Jobs Evaluation and Admin Observability
slices.goal: Add management commands, evaluation suites, admin actions, and operator visibility for memory sync and retrieval safety.
forbidden_moves: Do not introduce Celery until a separate production scheduling task accepts that dependency.
Do not expose raw PII snapshots through admin or UI.
Do not store temporary eval reports in the repository root.
acceptance_criteria: Management commands expose dry-run/help modes.
memory_eval includes smoke, PII, secret, and forbidden-scope checks.
Admin shows sources, jobs, blocked snapshots, and retrieval audit.
Operator cannot view raw PII without explicit future permission model.

tasks.goal: Add memory management commands, evaluation checks, and admin observability.
implementation_notes: Start with management commands and Django admin; defer Celery and rich UI.
Use dry-run support for sync/reindex commands.
Keep generated eval outputs in data/memory/eval/ or .local/.
escalate_if: Scheduling requires Celery or another production worker decision
Admin visibility requires a new permission model
Evaluation requires real patient data
